items.title: Man who fatally shot officer Keith Smith sentenced to 40 years in jail

children.content: Tasmanian man Leigh Sushames shot dead Keith Smith last year when the police constable tried to serve him with a home repossession notice.

items.title: Man suing Spirit of Tasmania operator told he 'should have sat' on ship's toilet

children.content: A man who claims to have sustained injuries after falling over while using a toilet at sea says the ferry operator was negligent, the federal court has heard.

image.altText: A large white and yellow cruise ship sits in the waterfront of a city.

items.title: Average annual Tasmanian power bill to rise by $100

children.content: Tasmanian households and businesses with the state-owned electricity retailer will be paying more for power, after a price rise was approved by the economic regulator.

image.altText: A worker in a safety helmet and vest uses equipment to manage overhead wires from an elevated platform.

items.title: Hobart stadium developer not supportive of waterfront precinct proposal

children.content: A proposal to build an underground car park, apartments and retail spaces neighbouring the Macquarie Point stadium site "does not appear to align with the precinct plan", stadium developer says.

gallery.altText: Renders of proposed housing, retail spaces and car parking at Hobart's Regatta Point by Regatta Quay Precinct.

an aerial shot of a riverside area showing a shaded area of ground for the proposed Regatta Quay Precinct

items.title: TassieCast: What's behind Tasmania's drop in vaccination rates?

children.content: Tasmania is experiencing a whooping cough epidemic. It's one of the highly contagious diseases that can be prevented through vaccination, but like nationally, fewer Tasmanians are keeping up to date with their jabs. Airlie Ward chats with reporter Selina Bryan.

items.title: Neil the seal's unique lifestyle may help protect him from bird flu

children.content: Experts say it is a matter of when, not if, a deadly strain of bird flu reaches Tasmania, and popular marine visitor Neil the seal is not isolated from the threat, despite his relatively solo lifestyle.

articles.title: Neil the seal is back bigger than ever and that's a problem

children.content: Neil, an elephant seal well known to Tasmanians, has returned to land and a new video shows him being coerced off a road for his own safety.

image.altText: Two people attempt to coax a large seal off a road.

gallery.altText: Neil the Seal, a juvenile elephant seal seen by a roadside in a suburban seaside neighbourhood.

thumbnail.altText: Neil the Seal, a juvenile elephant seal seen by a roadside in a suburban seaside neighbourhood.

items.title: Another suspected H5 bird flu case detected in WA

children.content: Another suspected positive case of the H5 bird flu has been detected in Western Australia, marking the fifth confirmed or suspected case in Australia.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length hair is speaking to media.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' charged with slavery offences is granted bail

children.content: A court grants bail for Melbourne grandmother Kawsar Ahmad, who is alleged to have owned a slave while in Syria under Islamic State rule.

image.altText: A woman wearing a brown hijab sits in court.

items.title: How China's new 'ethnic unity' law could target people in Australia

children.content: The Australian government raises concerns directly with China over the country's new ethnic unity law, which allows Beijing to target people overseas.

gallery.altText: Three women in ethnic minority outfits smile outside the Great Hall of the People.
10 people stand side by side wearing different ethnic minority outfits.

thumbnail.altText: Three women in ethnic minority outfits smile outside the Great Hall of the People.

items.title: Battle lost for Zempilas and WA Liberals in hope of winning war ahead

children.content: Basil Zempilas has had his first brush with the political truism "disunity is death" after MP Jonathan Huston quit the WA Liberal Party, but he's unlikely — at this stage at least — to be the one left in a political grave.

image.altText: Basil Zempilas is speaking to media with Jonathan Huston in the background.

items.title: 'An outright lie': Minister challenges Butler on Thriving Kids rollout

children.content: A member of the Thriving Kids advisory panel has voiced concern about how the support program for children with autism and developmental delay is being rolled out.

image.altText: A close up of a kindergartener's hands on a table

items.title: Dwayne Johnson and Catherine Lagaʻaia hit the Moana blue carpet

children.content: Dwayne Johnson and Sydneysider Catherine Lagaʻaia have walked the blue carpet at a special event in Sydney. They told ABC News why now is the perfect time for a live action Moana.

items.title: How I save money by storing and cooking things differently

children.content: I've developed simple storage and cooking systems that keep food visible and fresh while ensuring I use things up. Here's how I make cooking for one feel more doable, affordable and lower-impact.

image.altText: Koren stands, smiling, in front of her pantry with glass bottles behind her stacked up

items.title: Supergirl shoots for the moon, but runs out of fuel

children.content: Australian Milly Alcock shines as Supergirl in the hero's first solo outing since 1984. If only the rest of the movie sparkled around her.

items.title: Friday news quiz: Neil the Seal, the teals party down and Trump drains his swamp

children.content: This week's news quiz features the usual assortment of political animals and regular animals, as well as other odd happenings from across the world.

items.title: Comparing Trump's Iran negotiations to what Obama already did

children.content: The Trump administration has been negotiating directly with Iran this week in an effort to reach a long-term peace agreement, but it has several hurdles to clear before it can better the deal the United States already had.

image.altText: A composite image of the two leaders in suits

items.title: ASX down as ACCC announces anti-price gouging law

children.content: The ASX has slipped into negative territory after lunch on Friday. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: H5N1 emerged in Asia 30 years ago. Here's what happened next

children.content: Experts say Asia's decades-long experience fighting bird flu has shown there is no magic bullet and that fragmented responses to the disease will fail.

items.title: Warning fire ants could kill thousands of pets a year

children.content: If Australia does not ramp up fire ant eradication efforts, the invasive species will cause billion-dollar economic impacts and dozens of deaths, a study finds.

items.title: Queensland to introduce new drink and drug driving laws to fix 'loophole'

children.content: The Crisafulli government has announced a crackdown on dangerous driving while doubling down on a "zero-tolerance" approach to drug driving, including for medical cannabis users.

image.altText: Deb Frecklington on a police motorbike.

items.title: 'Incredibly special moment': Australian underdog makes it to Wimbledon

children.content: Australia's Dane Sweeny makes it through to an overseas grand slam for the first time after a hard-fought victory in the Wimbledon qualifiers.

gallery.altText: A tennis player pumps his fist and screams in joy after winning a point.

items.title: The Dolphins-Warriors game is the NRL's unlikeliest blockbuster

children.content: In a season bereft of quality, the Dolphins and the Warriors will meet in a marquee clash that doubles as the NRL's most unusual big game showdown.

items.title: Essendon announces selection panel to pick next AFL coach

children.content: Essendon has opted to go in-house with the panel that will decide whether James Hird returns as its AFL senior coach.

image.altText: James Hird, Director of Coaching at Port Melbourne speaks to players at the huddle

items.title: SA Police officers 'did not act appropriately' in handling of DV survivor's case

children.content: South Australia Police has admitted that three officers did not "act appropriately" in how they handled the case of domestic violence victim Stella Magnisalis, who was assaulted multiple times by her then police detective husband.

gallery.altText: A woman looks to the right sadly in front of an SA Police logo

thumbnail.altText: A woman looks to the right sadly in front of an SA Police logo

items.title: Matthew Guy says he's owed apology over Moira Deeming assault allegation

children.content: Victoria Police close an investigation into allegations state MP Matthew Guy assaulted fellow Liberal Moira Deeming at a community event, determining there was "there was no offence detected".

image.altText: A man in a suit stands looking sad.

items.title: Police watchdog orders misconduct investigation after gay club drug raids

children.content: Sydney MP Alex Greenwich said the investigation call by the Enforcement Conduct Commission was a "welcome first step".

image.altText: Oxford Street signs and neon motifs in Sydney.

items.title: King Charles won't move into Buckingham palace, breaking royal tradition

children.content: An annual financial statement released on Thursday contained several revelations, including plans to cut the royal family's public funding, and details of how much tax the monarch paid.

image.altText: A man looking on and smiling, wearing formal dress.

items.title: Terminally ill 72-year-old still chasing solo sailing dreams

children.content: David Burston had only just bought an old yacht to fix up and sail around Australia when he was diagnosed with stage 4 cancer. Hee has decided to keep pursuing his dream for as long as he can.

image.altText: Older man wearing sunglasses standing next to boat

items.title: Mixed reaction to beach town at forefront of over-50s lifestyle resort boom

children.content: Four over-50s lifestyle developments have been approved for this small beach community, with two more being assessed. Some worry the area is not ready for the influx, others say it is the answer to the housing crisis.
